rulings.Case Title: THOMAS RUBINO et al VS. AC AND S, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR WEDNESDAY, APRIL 27, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 AM, LINE 1. Imai, Tadlock, Keeney & Cordery, LLP¿s motion to be relieved as counsel for defendant The C.P. Hall Co. is granted. If defendant does not retain other counsel, plaintiffs may seek to strike defendant¿s answer and have a default entered against defendant. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: GERALD BURR SR VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR WEDNESDAY, APRIL 27, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 AM, LINE 2. Defendant Metalclad Insulation Corp.¿s motion for summary adjudication is granted as to issues 1, 2 and 5 (general negligence, strict liability design and manufacturing defect and punitive damages) and taken off calendar as to issues 3 and 4 (false representation and premises owner/contractor liability). Defendant has satisfied its burden of production of making a prima facie case that it is entitled to prevail on its government contractor defense as to non-duty to warn claims and plaintiff has not presented admissible evidence creating a triable issue on this defense. Defendant has satisfied its burden of production that plaintiff does not have and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that defendant acted with malice, fraud or oppression by clear and convincing evidence and plaintiff has not presented admissible evidence creating a triable issue that he is entitled to punitive damages. Plaintiff has dismissed his claims for false representation and premises owner/contractor liability. At the outset of the hearing the court will provide tentative rulings on the parties¿ evidentiary objections. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: SHERMAN FONG VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS B*P AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR WEDNESDAY, APRIL 27, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 AM, LINE 9. Defendant Metalclad Insulation Corp.¿s motion for summary adjudication is granted as to issues 1, 2, 4 and 5 (general negligence, strict liability design and manufacturing defect, civil battery and punitive damages) and taken off calendar as to issue 3 (false representation). Defendant has satisfied its burden of production of making a prima facie case that it is entitled to prevail on its government contractor defense as to non-duty to warn claims and plaintiff has not presented admissible evidence creating a triable issue on this defense. Defendant has satisfied its burden of production that plaintiffs do not have and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that defendant committed civil battery and/or acted with malice, fraud or oppression by clear and convincing evidence and plaintiffs have not presented admissible evidence creating a triable issue on their claim for civil battery or request for punitive damages. Plaintiffs represents that they dismissed their claim for false representation. At the outset of the hearing the court will provide tentative rulings on the parties¿ evidentiary objections. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JESSIE DUENAS VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR WEDNESDAY, APRIL 27, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 AM, LINE 3. Defendant Yarway Corp.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. No opposition filed. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: FRANK DELAHAYE VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR WEDNESDAY, APRIL 27, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 AM, LINE 4. Defendant Metalclad Insulation Corp.¿s motion for summary adjudication is granted as to issues 1, 2 and 5 (general negligence, strict liability design and manufacturing defect and punitive damages) and taken off calendar as to issues 3 and 4 (false representation and civil battery). Defendant has satisfied its burden of production of making a prima facie case that it is entitled to prevail on its government contractor defense as to non-duty to warn claims and plaintiff has not presented admissible evidence creating a triable issue on this defense. Defendant has satisfied its burden of production that plaintiff does not have and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that defendant acted with malice, fraud or oppression by clear and convincing evidence and plaintiff has not presented admissible evidence creating a triable issue that he is entitled to punitive damages. Plaintiff represents that he will dismiss his claims for false representation and civil battery. At the outset of the hearing the court will provide tentative rulings on the parties¿ evidentiary objections. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: SAMUEL FRANKLIN II et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion Of Hartford Fire Insurance Company, West American Insurance Company, Safeco Insurance Company Of America, Travelers Indemnity Co., And Zurich American Insurance Co., Successor In Interest To Zurich Insurance Company, Us Branch On Behalf Of Their Insured, Defendant Rich-Tex, Inc. To Dismiss For Failure To Prosecute Within 5 Years
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR WEDNESDAY, APRIL 27, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 AM, LINE 7. Hartford Fire Insurance Co. et al¿s motion to dismiss for failure to prosecute within 5 years is granted. No opposition filed. Hearing required unless counsel for the moving parties informs plaintiffs of this tentative ruling and they state that they don¿t wish to contest. = (220/HEK)
